cards.meaning: once; in the event that (a hypothetical, conditional future)
cards.example: 一旦封锁生效，油价就会上涨。
cards.examplePinyin: yīdàn fēngsuǒ shēngxiào, yóujià jiù huì shàngzhǎng
cards.exampleMeaning: Once the blockade takes effect, oil prices will rise.
cards.note: HSK-4 advanced connective; register leans 书面语/formal — a finance-desk word. Signature pattern: 一旦……就…… (condition first, then 就 introduces the result). Near-synonym contrast vs 一……就…… (yī…jiù…): 一……就…… is a REAL, immediate sequence ('the moment I arrive, I'll call'); 一旦 is an ABSTRACT hypothesis that hasn't happened yet ('in the event it happens, the consequences are serious').
cards.tiesTo: the Strait of Hormuz blockade and the conditional Fed rate-hike debate — 'once oil climbs, hikes are back on the table'

cards.example: 今天油价剧烈波动，黄金也创了新高。
cards.examplePinyin: jīntiān yóujià jùliè bōdòng, huángjīn yě chuàng le xīn gāo
cards.exampleMeaning: Oil prices swung violently today, and gold hit a new high too.
cards.note: HSK-4 abstract collocation, formal/market register. Portable: 市场波动 (market volatility), 剧烈波动 (violent swings), 价格波动 (price fluctuation). Near-synonym contrast vs 动荡 (dòngdàng): 波动 is a NEUTRAL up-and-down of prices/rates/data; 动荡 is heavier — social or political turmoil/unrest. So the market has 波动; the Middle East has 动荡.
cards.tiesTo: the violent swings in oil and the record in gold as Hormuz re-escalates

cards.meaning: a storm, a tempest; figuratively, a great upheaval or turmoil
cards.example: Sa gitna ng sigwa ng merkado, kailangan ng malamig na ulo.
cards.exampleMeaning: In the middle of the market's turmoil, you need a cool head.
cards.note: Native-speaker distinction from bagyo: bagyo is literal — the meteorological system itself; sigwa is more figurative and charged — a tempest, and by extension a social or political upheaval (the 1970 'First Quarter Storm' protests are called the Sigwa ng Unang Sangkapat). Reach for sigwa when you mean fury or upheaval, not just weather.
cards.tiesTo: the habagat soaking Luzon and the tempest in the markets

cards.meaning: capital; one's stake or investment in something
cards.example: Inilipat ng mga kliyente ang kanilang puhunan mula sa software patungo sa data center.
cards.exampleMeaning: Customers shifted their capital out of software and into the data center.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance vs kapital: kapital is purely monetary and technical, a Spanish loanword; puhunan is broader and more native — it can be money, time, trust, or effort ('namuhunan siya' = she invested). The rich sense carries social and personal stake, not just cash on a balance sheet.
cards.tiesTo: the AI capex buildout ($725B hyperscaler spend) and IBM's customers redirecting their spend
